diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A dry non-productive cough is characteristic of radiation-induced alveolitis.
Absence of purulent sputum makes typical bacterial pneumonia somewhat less likely but does not exclude atypical infection.
Impaired gas exchange from alveolitis produces worsening dyspnoea; orthopnoea reflects reduced reserve.
Orthopnoea is also cardiac; must be considered until unilateral signs and absent oedema argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Completion of thoracic radiotherapy ~10 weeks prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The 6-12 week post-radiotherapy window is the defining temporal risk factor for radiation pneumonitis.
Active malignancy under treatment is a strong prothrombotic state, keeping PE on the differential.
Low-grade fever accompanies the inflammatory alveolitis of pneumonitis.
Fever keeps infection actively in the differential; it cannot be excluded remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute exacerbation of asthma or COPD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bronchodilator with zero effect argues strongly against a bronchoconstrictive cause.
An inflammatory alveolar process does not respond to beta-2 agonists.
Hypoxia reflects impaired gas exchange; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth but unverified and single-reading.
Unexplained resting hypoxia in a cancer patient is a red flag for PE.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased work of breathing / accessory-muscle use / speaking in short phrases

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Video-observable respiratory distress marks reduced reserve and mandates urgent escalation irrespective of the underlying cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised fine inspiratory crackles confined to the right upper lobe matching the radiation field

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Crackles conforming to the radiation port rather than an anatomical lobar boundary are highly specific — but auscultation is impossible in telehealth; this is a sealed clinician measurement.
A geometric radiation-port distribution fits pneumonitis rather than a lobar infective consolidation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation dermatitis / hyperpigmentation over the right anterior chest wall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Skin changes mark the radiation field and corroborate the treated volume; visible on video but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-resolution chest CT with geometric ground-glass opacities matching the radiation port

investigation_details.expected_result: Ground-glass and reticular opacities in a non-anatomical, geometric distribution conforming to the radiation port
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging, radiation pneumonitis cannot be confirmed and PE/infection cannot be excluded — the definitive diagnostic step is unavailable in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The geometric, port-conforming opacity distribution is the diagnostic gold standard — obtainable only in person, never via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haemoptysis and no purulent sputum (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent purulent sputum reduces the likelihood of typical bacterial pneumonia but does not exclude atypical infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical assessment with pulse oximetry, chest auscultation and observations
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Resting hypoxia and respiratory distress require hands-on assessment that telehealth cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: High-resolution CT chest (and consideration of CT pulmonary angiogram)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms radiation pneumonitis distribution and, with CTPA, excludes pulmonary embolism in a prothrombotic cancer patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest X-ray and inflammatory markers / FBE
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Helps distinguish infection from pneumonitis; neither can be assessed remotely.
